results.name: Harvard University
results.key_finding: DOJ suit to recoup grants + ~$2.2B freeze (court-blocked; prior ruling found the cut unlawful) is federal action AGAINST Harvard - attributed to perpetrator, not a Harvard compassion failure. Continued layoffs/salary-freeze/Broad cuts are a real I3/B1 staff-harm signal but consistent with already-low INT 2.5/ACC 2.75; bearing cost vs settling is an offsetting positive I1. Refinement holds at 52.3 Functional.

results.name: Columbia University
results.key_finding: $221M settlement ($200M fine + $21M EEOC fund, claims close Jun 2) is externally-imposed; punitive protest discipline and cross-group safety failures driving low EMP 2.5/INT 2.25 were already in the assembly rationale; UAW strike threat already priced. Refinement holds at 44.5 Functional.

results.name: Brown University
results.key_finding: Jul 2025 $50M workforce-development settlement with first $1.5M grants paid Jan 2026 (CCRI, Building Futures) is a comparatively constructive, community-directed resolution consistent with Brown's strongest-in-cohort SYS 3.5/EQU 3.75. No adverse new conduct. Holds at 60.2 Established.

results.name: New York University
results.key_finding: GSOC grad-worker strike win (Apr 2026) and contract-faculty unionization (89%) are worker-voice WINS, not compassion failures; contested felt-care already reflected in low EMP 2.5/INT 2.25. Holds at 39.1 Developing.

results.name: Yale University
results.key_finding: 19 students arrested Mar 2026 at fossil-fuel divestment protest is a modest enforcement-over-care signal; the dominant documented harm (Elis for Rachel disability-leave withdrawal-pressure suit) already drives EMP/INT scores. Sub-threshold; holds at 55.5 Functional.

results.name: Stanford University
results.key_finding: DOJ Civil Rights admissions compliance review + NSF cuts are external action AGAINST Stanford; no new entity-specific conduct event. Holds at 54.7 Functional.

results.name: University of Southern California
results.key_finding: NLRB allowed ~2,900 RTPC faculty to unionize and UF-UAW won (>1,200 in favor) amid university opposition; the catastrophic Tyndall detection/validation failure + OCR finding driving low ACC 2.0/EMP 2.25/INT 2.0 remain dominant. University anti-union pushback is a minor sub-threshold I3 negative. Holds at 36.7 Developing.

results.name: University of California, Berkeley
results.key_finding: DOJ admissions review, Brandeis Center suit settled Mar 2026, system-level PERB complaint - external pressure plus system-level labor; no Berkeley-specific new conduct harm. Holds at 55.5 Functional.

results.name: University of California, Irvine
results.key_finding: DOJ admissions compliance review names UCI; no entity-specific event in window. Strong equity profile (EQU 4.5, first-gen-serving) intact. Holds at 60.2 Established.

results.name: University of California, Los Angeles
results.key_finding: $1.2B federal fine sought against UCLA is external action AGAINST UCLA; 21 unions/faculty orgs sued the administration. 2024 encampment handling (delayed protection, mass arrest) driving low ACC 2.25 already in rationale. Holds at 45.3 Functional.

results.name: Apple
results.key_finding: IAM NLRB ULP charge over the Jun 20 Towson unionized-store closure (~78-90 workers; alleged transfer-rights disparity), backed by 54 Labor Caucus members and MD Gov. Moore. A real I3 signal but a single pre-adjudication contested charge against Apple's broad published responsibility infrastructure. Same finding confirmed sub-threshold 2026-06-19. Holds at 59.4; on I3 watch.

results.name: UnitedHealth Group
results.key_finding: DOJ criminal probe expanded to Optum Rx and physician reimbursement; Senate (Grassley) found UHG 'aggressively' gaming Medicare Advantage risk scores. Reinforces near-floor Critical and upgrades ACC/INT/SYS evidence tier, but is pre-adjudication (no charges/settlement) and UHG already at 10.2 with minimal headroom. Near-floor limitation; no delta. Re-flag on indictment/settlement.

results.name: Nigeria
results.key_finding: Yesterday's Developing->Critical downgrade to 18.0 (FAO-WFP/IPC Phase 5 Catastrophe for ~15,000 in Borno; 34.8M at Phase 3+) was applied to the live index. Today's scan re-surfaces the same FAO-WFP Jun 17 evidence; no new scorable downside. Now at near-floor Critical; confirms at 18.0.

results.name: Ukraine
results.key_finding: Jun 2/18 strikes and UN 'highest civilian-fatality month in 3 years' (16,126 verified killed) are harm ON Ukraine BY Russia - victim attribution to the perpetrator. Ukraine own-profile unchanged. Confirms at 50.0 Functional.

results.name: Israel
results.key_finding: IDF expanding to ~70% of Gaza, UNRWA blocked from direct aid since Mar 2025, 76,000 in collective shelters, HRW 2026 World Report confirms systematic aid-blocking, 1,103 West Bank Palestinians killed (241 children). Harm attributed to Israel. Already at absolute harm-flag floor (0.0); reinforces, cannot lower.

results.name: Bolivia
results.key_finding: Week 8+ protests: 9 dead (6 from blocked ambulance access; cancer patient died unable to reach radiotherapy), emergency powers in force, 103+ blockades, La Paz 90-day sanitary emergency, army authorized but NOT deployed. Same enabling-framework basis behind the applied 6.3. Near floor; no downside room.

results.name: Marathon Oil
results.key_finding: Rotation backfill; no fresh compassion-relevant adverse evidence in window. Confirms at placeholder-grade Developing 23.4 (low-confidence desk review).

results.name: Olin Corporation
results.key_finding: Rotation backfill; no fresh adverse evidence. Confirms at placeholder-grade Developing 23.4 (low-confidence desk review).

results.name: Mosaic
results.key_finding: Rotation backfill; no fresh adverse evidence. Confirms at placeholder-grade Developing 23.4 (low-confidence desk review).

results.name: Triumph Group
results.key_finding: Rotation backfill; no fresh adverse evidence. Confirms at placeholder-grade Developing 23.4 (low-confidence desk review).

results.name: Dollar Tree
results.key_finding: Rotation backfill; no fresh adverse evidence in window (longstanding OSHA citation history already consistent with placeholder Developing). Confirms at 23.4.

notes: 21 entities assessed (16 priority + 5 rotation backfill). ZERO change proposals: every assessed entity confirms at its live published composite (delta 0.0). Universities (10 assessed: Harvard, Columbia, Brown, NYU, Yale, Stanford, USC, UC Berkeley, UC Irvine, UCLA) treated as REFINEMENTS of their index-assembly baselines, NOT first baselines. The June-2026 university news cluster is overwhelmingly (a) federal action INFLICTED ON the universities (DOJ funding suits, grant freezes, EEOC settlements, admissions compliance reviews) - attributed to the perpetrator (federal government) under victim/perpetrator rule, mirroring Ukraine's treatment; (b) labor organizing WINS/settlements (NYU GSOC, USC RTPC, Columbia UAW) - worker-voice gains, not compassion failures; (c) protest discipline and Title IX/OCR history (Columbia, UCLA, USC-Tyndall, Yale-leave) already explicitly cited in assembly rationales and reflected in published EMP/ACC/INT. No university crosses the 5-pt threshold; all published composites HOLD. The one genuinely new conduct signal - continued Harvard layoffs/salary-freeze/Broad Institute cuts (I3/B1 staff-harm) - is consistent with Harvard's already-low INT 2.5/ACC 2.75 and is partially offset by a positive I1 (bearing cost rather than settling); net zero. Apple (Towson NLRB ULP) confirmed sub-threshold for the second consecutive night - real I3 signal, single pre-adjudication charge vs broad infrastructure; on I3 watch. UnitedHealth (10.2 near-floor Critical) reinforced by the DOJ criminal-probe expansion to Optum Rx + Senate Medicare-gaming finding but near-floor limitation applies (pre-adjudication, minimal headroom); re-flag on indictment/settlement. Nigeria's Developing->Critical downgrade to 18.0 was already applied to the live index 2026-06-19; today re-surfaces the same FAO-WFP evidence with no new downside - confirms at 18.0 near-floor Critical. Floor-limitation applied to Israel (0.0 harm-flag floor) and Bolivia (6.3 near-floor). Ukraine harm attributed to Russia (victim, not perpetrator) - confirmed 50.0. Sudan/DRC/Iran appeared in sector alerts only (already assessed 2026-06-19, at/near floor) - reinforced via the confirmations file, not re-scored. Canonical-formula (computeCompositeFromDimensions, v1.2) reconstruction of all 12 spot-checked baselines (10 universities + Apple + UHG) matched published EXACTLY (diff 0.0). No math-hygiene flags. Rotation-state updated for all 21 entities (last_assessed=2026-06-20); no last_change_proposal updates (no proposals). Outputs: research/assessments/confirmations-2026-06-20.md, research/assessments/harvard-university-2026-06-20.md, research/assessments/unitedhealth-group-2026-06-20.md.
